$notes: Hybrid of v0.1 parametric engine + academic cofounder's pedagogy/visual/feedback model.
RULE: every v0.1 field is preserved. New fields are OPTIONAL with defaults.
RULE: existing 480 questions validate unchanged against v0.2.

example_parent_question.__NEW__ pedagogy_notes: // enum: cpa_concrete | cpa_pictorial | cpa_abstract | drill | exploration

__NEW__ curriculum_alignment.__notes: // Structured tags replacing loose 'tags' list for curriculum mapping

example_parent_question.stem_template: Look at the {scene}. How many {adjective} {object} do you see?

__NEW__ interaction.__notes: // Replaces answer_type long-term. Backward compat: if missing, infer from answer_type.

__NEW__ visual_manifest.__notes: // Human-readable brief for illustration pipeline. Used when svg_generator doesn't exist or for AI-generated art.
__NEW__ visual_manifest.art_brief: A {scene} with {N} bright {adjective} {object} scattered across it. Each {object} should be distinct and easy to tap.

misconceptions.feedback_child: Almost! It looks like one got counted twice. Try pointing to each one just once.
So close! Check if you missed one hiding in the corner.
Let's slow down and count together, one by one.

__NEW__ socratic_feedback.__notes: // Category-based feedback layer ON TOP of misconceptions. Misconceptions handle specific wrong answers; socratic handles patterns the formula can't predict (tap interactions, partial attempts, etc.)
__NEW__ socratic_feedback.on_correct: Amazing! You counted {N} {object}. Now they're ready for the picnic!
__NEW__ socratic_feedback.on_partial: You're getting there! Keep counting — what number comes after {attempt}?
__NEW__ socratic_feedback.on_timeout: Take your time! Start from the left and point to each {object} one at a time.
__NEW__ socratic_feedback.encouragement_after_retry: You've got this! Remember, touch each one only once.

example_parent_question.author: AP

new_optional_fields: pedagogy         — enum, CPA stage or drill/exploration. Default: null (inferred from tier).
curriculum_alignment — structured object replacing loose tags for framework mapping.
interaction       — richer interaction spec with type + target_formula + fallback. If missing, infer from answer_type.

visual_manifest   — human-readable art brief with template vars. Coexists with visual (svg_generator). Renderer prefers svg_generator if available, falls back to visual_manifest for AI-gen or static art.
socratic_feedback — category-based feedback layer for non-formula-predictable mistakes (partial taps, timeouts, encouragement).
what_changed_from_v01.breaking_changes: NONE. All new fields are Optional with null defaults. Existing 480 questions pass validation unchanged.
deprecations: answer_type — kept but superseded by interaction.type when present. Will remove in v0.3.
tags — kept but curriculum-specific tags should migrate to curriculum_alignment.

rendering_priority.__notes: How the Flutter app decides what to show for visuals
rendering_priority.1_svg_generator: If visual.type == 'svg_generator' AND generator exists in backend → render SVG inline (fastest, crispest).
rendering_priority.2_visual_manifest: If svg_generator missing/unknown → use visual_manifest.art_brief to render via AI illustration pipeline or pre-rendered asset lookup.
rendering_priority.3_alt_text_only: If both missing → show alt_text as accessible fallback, no visual card.
rendering_priority.4_nothing: If no visual and no visual_manifest → hide visual card entirely (current behavior).

v02_additions: tap_to_count    — child taps objects in visual, app counts taps. Needs visual_manifest or interactive SVG.
multiple_choice_visual — options are images not text. Needs option visuals.
drag_to_order   — child drags items into sequence. Replaces 'drag_and_drop' with clearer semantics.
numeric_keypad  — child types a number. Replaces 'numeric_input' with clearer name.
interaction_types_v02.rule: Frontend must have a renderer for the type before content authors can use it. fallback_type ensures graceful degradation.
